title: Foundations & Design
tui: v1.0 · 2013-2016 · MediaSuite Co-op + BugsLife - web design, UX ownership, custom payment & scheduling systems.
summary: Started as a co-op at MediaSuite, independently designing and shipping 50+ custom client websites. Simultaneously launched BugsLife Solutions doing freelance web and mobile work for local businesses. Built real product instincts early by owning the full design-to-delivery loop without a senior layer above.
highlights: Designed and built 50+ uniquely designed client websites at MediaSuite with full creative ownership
Built a custom shopping cart and checkout solution for a client - no off-the-shelf compromise
Developed a swimming lesson registration platform with multi-child management, scheduling logic, digital waivers, and recurring payments
Founded BugsLife Solutions and began delivering web and mobile projects for local businesses
Owned every UX decision: layout, typography, visual hierarchy, and information architecture

title: Frontend & Integration
tui: v2.0 · 2017 · Geek Power + OrderEase - React, Angular, REST APIs, .NET, CI/CD pipelines.
summary: Two roles back-to-back that pushed deeply into frontend frameworks and backend integration engineering. At Geek Power, led modernization of legacy systems using React and Angular. At OrderEase, built REST API endpoints for large B2B clients and a .NET GUI tool enabling non-technical staff to sync local databases with cloud systems.
highlights: Built custom UX solutions using Angular and React as lead developer on high-detail client projects
Modernized legacy codebases for enterprise clients at Geek Power Web Design
Designed and tested REST API endpoints used by large B2B agricultural supply clients at OrderEase
Built a Windows .NET GUI tool allowing non-technical staff to sync local inventory databases with cloud APIs
Managed KANBAN boards, GitHub workflows, and CI pipelines at OrderEase

title: Mobile & Systems
tui: v3.0 · 2018-2019 · Georgian College + BugsLife mobile - Flutter, Firebase, Bluetooth beacons, native integrations.
summary: Worked at Georgian College resolving complex data integration issues between marketing systems and public-facing web platforms. Concurrently, BugsLife delivered its most technically ambitious project: the Group of Seven Trail App - a Flutter mobile app with Bluetooth proximity triggers, C++ native layers, and offline trail plotting. This era solidified mobile-first, systems-level engineering skills.
highlights: Designed and built the Group of Seven Trail App - Flutter + Bluetooth beacon proximity triggers + C++ native layers
Built a custom CMS solution enabling hikers to access Group of Seven painting site content off-site
Resolved complex term-data discrepancies between IT and marketing systems at Georgian College
Translated non-technical marketing requirements into functional web integrations
Ensured accurate real-time display of course and enrollment data for students across Georgian College platforms

title: Full Stack & DevOps
tui: v4.0 · 2019-Present · How To Hockey (Lead) + BugsLife - full stack ownership, production infra, CI/CD, Flutter mobile.
summary: Joined How To Hockey as the primary full stack and DevOps lead for web and mobile platforms serving hockey players worldwide. Owned everything: Flutter mobile apps, Firebase architecture, CI/CD pipelines, production stability, and real-time community tools. Every technical decision starts with the same question - as a user, what would I expect right here?
highlights: Sole software and DevOps lead for How To Hockey - full ownership across frontend, backend, mobile, and infrastructure
Built and shipped the 10,000 Shots Flutter mobile app including in-app purchases and real-time Firebase leaderboards
Designed real-time stat aggregation and team leaderboard systems with live Firebase data streaming
Implemented automated website backups, system snapshots, and recovery pipelines for production resilience
Built and maintained CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions - stable, repeatable, zero-manual-intervention releases
Shipped NextShift - a real-time community feedback and voting platform built at How To Hockey
